Jan  2 21:04:10 lelu kernel: umass0: Invalid CSW: tag 1953024 should be 1953025
Jan  2 21:04:10 lelu kernel: umass0: Invalid CSW: tag 1953025 should be 1953026
Jan  2 21:04:11 lelu kernel: umass0: Invalid CSW: tag 1953026 should be 1953027
Jan  2 21:04:11 lelu kernel: umass0: Invalid CSW: tag 1953027 should be 1953028

Any of the USB or Firewire disks start emitting various umass errors.
Usually the kernel gets increasingly confused, and a panic or lockup
results.   

Notable things:

- This happens on multiple computers, the slow one above, and amd64 board.

- The problem does not seem to be USB/Firewire specific, or disk
specific.  I have disks from Lacie (several), maxtor.  All of the fail
with same symptoms, if properly teased.  The disks are also hanging
from different USB controllers in most cases.

- It is triggered by heavy I/O, in particular random access.

- Usually the problem starts with various errors in log files, then a
particular error like the one above, then the disk either locks
up, or the computer panics.  Panics seem to vary so it probably is
just indication of the something else failed already.

- When one disks locks up mode, umount of any other filesystem, even
IDE/ATA disks, locks up as well.  This is annoying as it results in
fsck on all disks. 

Other picks from messages:
